title: U-shaped LapTime_Delta binning and middle-stability suppressor
summary: Model LapTime_Delta as a nonlinear U-shaped signal: both very negative and very positive deltas can precede pits, while stable middle laps suppress pit risk.
rationale: The deep-dive EDA reports near-zero linear correlation for LapTime_Delta but a clear decile pattern: extremes carry signal, while middle bins are low-risk. Existing features use raw, absolute, and positive-part transforms, but the notebook-specific insight is the low-risk middle stability band plus two-sided extremes.
implementation_hint: Within folds, create quantile bins or spline features for LapTime_Delta, signed extreme flags, middle-stability flags, and interactions with TyreLife, Compound, RaceProgress, and recent pit-wave state. Compare against raw/absolute/squared-only transforms.
expected_effect: Could improve borderline pit-window ranking where linear models or trees underuse the symmetric extreme-lap signal.
risk: Extreme deltas may encode safety-car/out-lap artifacts rather than pit pressure; validate by race-group folds and avoid bins with tiny support.
